AI technology enables malicious actors to automate, scale, and optimize cyberattacks, such as phishing, malware generation, password cracking, and social engineering. This results in faster, more effective, and harder-to-detect attacks, significantly amplifying the overall threat landscape.

AI systems themselves can introduce new attack surfaces, including susceptibility to adversarial attacks where manipulated inputs deceive AI models. Poorly secured AI systems may also be targeted to disrupt services or exfiltrate sensitive information, thereby increasing the scope of cybersecurity threats.
